Time to harvest what we've covered so far with a little exercise. I like to drop a small practice project every few chapters so this series isn't just reading about Python — building something tiny makes it stick. This time it's the classic BMI calculator, written first in JavaScript and then in Python, plus a look at Python's string formatting.

Next up: conditionals and loops, since you basically can't write any program without these two. We'll line up JavaScript's if/else against Python's if/elif/else, talk about the new match statement in Python 3.10, and cover for and while loops including how to fake a do...while in Python.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (6) - Variable Scope
iThome Ironman Contest 13th

Last time we covered functions and variables, so now I want to circle back and fill in something I skipped: variable scope. We'll look at how Python's scoping differs from JavaScript's var/let/const, why Python behaves a lot like var, whether Python has a scope chain, and what nonlocal and global are actually for.

Okay, we're finally writing some actual Python. We've messed around for three days now, and if I don't start soon people are going to unsubscribe. Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (3): Installing It

Next up is actually installing Python — because if we want to learn Python, we kind of need Python on our machine first. The install looks trivial, but Windows and macOS each have their own quirks and a couple of gotchas, so I'm giving both of them their own walkthrough here.
